Motion & Notice Of Hearing To Change The Place Of Trial
Off Calendar Aug-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KATE HERSH-BOYLE OF THE HERSH & HERSH, TEL. NO. 1-415-441-5544 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LYNNE M. COATES OF THE SHEURMAN, MARTIN, TABARI, TEL. NO. 1-408-288-9700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH;
ATTY. BARRY C. MARSH OF HINSHAW MARSH, TEL. NO. 1-408-861-6500 APPEARING FOR PAFMG;
ATTY. ROBERT WILL

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S Motion To Change The Place Of Trial is DENIED. It is undisputed that Defendant Dignity Health's principal place of business is in San Francisco, establishing a statutory basis for venue. A change of venue on the ground that the change will be more convenient for witnesses is in the sound discretion of the trial court. (Churchill v. White (1953) 119 Cal.App.2d 503, 506.) Tort claims against a corporation may be tried in any county in which the liability arises as well as in the county where the corporation's principal place of business is located. (Cal. Code Civ. Pro. 395.5.) Defendant submits one declaration to support its motion, providing the names, occupations, and residence of seven witnesses involved with Plaintiff's medical treatment. Defendant's declaration, however does not state with sufficient specificity why the witnesses will be inconvenienced. Under the circumstances of this case, the convenience of witnesses for Defendant does not support an order for change of venue. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489
